Class DelayedExecution

java.lang.Object
org.efs.util.DelayedExecution
public final class DelayedExecution extends Object
This class provides static methods which perform a Runnable task after a specified delay. This delay and execution are performed on the caller's thread. This means the caller is blocked until the execution is completed.

Note: timing is at millisecond granularity. Any delay < one millisecond will be treated as zero milliseconds.

This utility class is targeted towards unit testing. While it appears to be a function provided by awaitility, it is not since awaitility is focused on waiting for a condition to be satisfied and not on task execution.

This functionality is not meant to replace org.efs.timer which provides scheduled execution for the IEfsAgents but only for simple scheduled task execution for unit tests.

Author:
Charles W. Rapp

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    NEGATIVE_DELAY
    When delay is < zero, an IllegalArgumentException is thrown with message "delay < zero".
    static final String
    NULL_DELAY
    When delay is null, a NullPointerException is thrown with message "delay is null".

    NULL_TASK
    When task is null, a NullPointerException is thrown with message "task is null".

  • Method Details

    • delayedExecution

      public static void delayedExecution(Duration delay, Runnable task)
      Executes given task after waiting specified millisecond delay. If delay < one millisecond, then task is executed immediately. Current thread is blocked until delay and task execution completes.

      Note: any exception thrown by task is allowed to flow back to caller.

      Parameters:
      delay - wait this duration before executing task.
      task - execute this delay after delay completes.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either delay or task is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if delay < zero.

    • waitUntil

      public static void waitUntil(Duration delay)
      Waits on the current thread until specified delay is reached. Current thread is blocked for this delay.
      Parameters:
      delay - wait this many milliseconds.
